Alachua County FHA Loans

FHA loans in Alachua County, Florida

FHA loans across Alachua County with as little as 3.5% down. Here's the county's 2026 FHA loan limit, why the Gainesville area suits FHA buyers, and how to get pre-approved.

Alachua County Home Loans

FHA loans in Alachua County, Florida

Alachua County is anchored by Gainesville, home to the University of Florida and a major medical hub. Its education-and-healthcare economy gives it stability and an affordable housing market, drawing first-time buyers, young professionals, and university and hospital staff. The FHA loan limit easily covers local prices, so 3.5% down opens the door across most of the county.

Alachua County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Alachua County

With the 2026 FHA floor of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a Alachua County home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). In Alachua County's affordable market, that covers the large majority of homes for sale. A handful of Florida counties are set higher, so we confirm Alachua County's exact figure with HUD.

A college and medical economy

Steady university and healthcare employment gives many Alachua County buyers the reliable income FHA looks for, and prices leave ample room under the loan limit — great for first-time buyers. Remember FHA is for primary residences you'll occupy, not student rentals. Our Gainesville city guide digs into neighborhoods, condos near campus, and local down payment help.

Communities We Serve

FHA loans across Alachua County

We help FHA buyers throughout Alachua County, including Gainesville, Alachua, and Newberry. For a closer look at the local market, loan limits, and down payment help, explore our city guide:

Gainesville

Down payment help in Alachua County

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Alachua County buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Alachua County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Alachua County for 2026?+
Alachua County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers most homes in the county. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Is Alachua County good for FHA buyers?+
Yes — stable university and medical employment plus affordable prices make Gainesville and Alachua County a strong FHA market.
Can I use FHA as a UF student or for a rental?+
FHA loans are for primary residences you'll live in, not pure investment rentals. If you'll occupy the home, FHA is a great fit.
How much down payment do I need in Alachua County?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, reduced with gift funds and Florida assistance.
Do you serve all of Alachua County?+
Yes — Gainesville, Alachua, Newberry, and every community in the county.
